items.step: Snapshot the current durable plan and replace it with the linear-DSL pivot graph
items.notes: Exported .step/st-plan.pre-linear-dsl.snapshot.json before import.

items.step: Define the new first-order public surface around Prompt, Step, Outcome, Pending, EscapedOwner, run, and suspend
items.notes: Prompt carries Request and Resume only; machine types carry Frame, Suspend, Resume, Answer, and Error.

items.step: Implement the first-order runtime executor over owned frame data with no native-stack continuation capture
items.notes: The owned continuation must be explicit data, not a hidden fiber or stack snapshot.

items.step: Implement delayed escape and prove deinit frees owned continuation data without re-entry
items.notes: This is the hard linearity witness for the new runtime.

items.step: Implement one-machine multi-prompt suspend and resume unions with ResumeMismatch validation
items.notes: Use one explicit Suspend union and one matching Resume union per machine.

items.step: Archive and remove the live native-body stackful reset and shift surface from the root package
items.notes: No compatibility shim; the old surface is archive-only.

items.step: Rewrite docs and examples around run and suspend and add one non-toy workflow example
items.notes: The workflow example must prove multi-prompt plus delayed escape in the explicit frame DSL.

items.step: Rebuild compile-fail, smoke, size, docs, and benchmark gates against the linear DSL
items.notes: Closure requires bench, bench-first-suspend, and bench-delayed-escape on the new linear runtime.

items.step: Delete the archived stackful runtime from the active repo surface once the linear DSL closure is green
items.notes: The archive may survive as historical source, but no active docs, build paths, or root exports may depend on it.

items.step: Add resolved Effect IR core with row normalization, output schema, and symbol/SCC resolver
items.notes: New src/effect_ir.zig added; local validation: zig test src/effect_ir.zig passes. Next semantic-center additions should preserve row/output normalization and SCC grouping.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: proof: zig test src/effect_ir.zig passed; core row/output/SCC file added at src/effect_ir.zig

items.step: Add root API for shift.Row, shift.Uses, shift.handle, shift.bind, direct call shapes, and shift.RunResult

items.step: Record the corrective successor wave and make the durable plan reflect the real remaining closure work
items.notes: Reason: st-309..st-312 closed on the explicit non-benchmark bundle, but zig build and repo-wide lint are still red because retired shipped custom surfaces remain active.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: proof: corrective successor wave recorded in $st and mirrored into update_plan before code edits resumed

items.step: Add a shipped open-row corpus registry, remove retired examples from the install graph, and land the transform/choice/abort replacements
items.notes: This step establishes the new shipped custom corpus boundary and makes zig build stop compiling the retired examples.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: progress: retired examples removed from zig build install graph; new open-row custom corpus files plus fixtures landed; zig build, example-proof, source-lowering-gauntlet, zig build test, and explicit non-lint proof bundle are green. registry cutover and lint remain.

items.step: Land the workflow, abortive-validation, artifact-search, and generator open-row replacements with fixtures and proof wiring
items.notes: The full rewritten custom corpus is required before the generalized-effects claim can be considered true.

items.step: Cut build, source-lowering, authoring, docs, tool contracts, and proof inventories over to the registry-backed open-row corpus and delete retired shipped custom examples
items.notes: No active shipped surface may retain retired custom/algebraic IDs or source paths after this step.

items.step: Trim legacy parity, bridge, and reference-only lanes down to the minimum renamed open-row truth required for the final claim
items.notes: Retain only proof lanes that still carry unique value after the open-row corpus replacement, and rename any retained lane onto the new truth.

items.step: Drive zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 to zero warnings and errors across the repo without suppressions or scope reduction
items.notes: Lint is part of the same closure campaign, not a later cleanup.

items.step: Rerun zig build, zig build test, zig build lint, and the explicit proof bundle, then update durable proof comments and close the campaign
items.notes: Closure requires zig build, zig build test, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, example-proof, source-lowering-gauntlet, public-error-api-ban, public-root-contract-snapshot-check, readme-contract, formal-core, and retired-lane-inventory-check.
